steps.title: Overview: Laser Geometry
steps.description: The layout tour ended with cut panels and an SVG document. This tour explains how those panels are shaped: edge profiles, finger holes, drawing primitives, the TypeScript Boxes layer, and parity scripts against the upstream Python generator.

steps.title: Shared Geometry
steps.description: The geometry type gathers the dimensions needed by both cutting and assembly. It keeps filter size, chamber height, depth, fan layers, and filter layers in one calculated object.

steps.title: Panel Drafts
steps.description: Panel drafts describe a panel before sheet placement is assigned. They already carry width, height, edge sections, features, and optional assembly role.

steps.title: Edge Sections
steps.description: Edges are split into typed sections. Finger, plain, and dovetail sections can live on the same side, which lets laser and print variants share panel outlines while changing connector strategy.

steps.title: Rectangular Panel Builder
steps.description: The rectangular panel builder turns dimensions and edge sections into an outline. Features are added separately, so holes and labels do not have to be encoded into the outer path.

steps.title: Finger Hole Slots
steps.description: Finger holes are generated as cut features along a span. This function handles count, spacing, and rotation, which makes slot placement deterministic enough to test.

steps.title: Boxes Document
steps.description: The Boxes layer is a small drawing kernel. It stores sheets, shapes, and operations in a browser-friendly representation before SVG rendering.

steps.title: Air Purifier Generator
steps.description: The ported generator creates walls and places finger holes with the same abstractions as the generic Boxes kernel. This path is used by compatibility tests and audit scripts.

steps.title: SVG Renderer
steps.description: SVG rendering receives a Boxes document and writes XML layers. The renderer is a final formatting step; geometry decisions already happened before this point.

steps.title: Port Oracle Script
steps.description: The oracle script compares local layout facts against the upstream Boxes.py implementation. It is a developer tool for checking the TypeScript port, not a production dependency.

steps.title: Quick Check
steps.text: Which structure is shared by the laser SVG path and the assembled preview path?
steps.explanation: `CutPanel` starts at line 89 in `src/fabrication/laser/cutGeometry.ts` and includes outline, features, sheet placement, and optional assembly metadata.

steps.title: Summary
steps.description: Laser geometry flows from calculated dimensions into typed panel outlines, features, drawing documents, and SVG. The next tour shows how those same panels become a physical assembly for the 3D preview.
